hypothesis: If language models fine-tuned on alloy data are further trained with Direct Preference Optimization using thermodynamic simulation–based pairwise rewards, then they will generate BCC/B2 alloy compositions with higher mean synthesis reward (improved BCC/B2 coexistence, correct phase ordering, room-temperature B2 stability, and ≤10% non-BCC/B2 phases), higher compositional validity, and improved uniqueness/novelty/coverage compared to SFT-only and baseline methods because DPO directly optimizes for physics-based preferences while constraining distribution shift.
